programmes.title: SMART Recovery
programmes.teaser: Self Management and Recovery Training — practical tools, peer support, and choice-led recovery.
programmes.location: Recovery Hub, 1 Connaught Road, Wolverhampton WV1 4SJ
programmes.referralNote: Contact SUIT for a referral to attend these meetings.

body.text: SMART (Self Management and Recovery Training) is a programme that provides training and tools for people who want to change problematic behaviour — including addiction to drugs, alcohol, cigarettes, gambling, food, shopping, the internet, and more.
Guided by trained facilitators, participants help themselves and each other using cognitive behaviour therapy (CBT) and motivational tools and techniques.
The 4-Point Programme
The four points are the foundation of SMART. Many of the tools can support your recovery and help you handle future challenges with more balance.
SMART is secular and advocates personal choice. Language like ‘addict’ and ‘alcoholic’ is avoided — people are not their behaviours. Everyone has the power to choose change.
You’re free to use other services or mutual-aid groups that help you; there is no single pathway. When life feels healthy and balanced, you can move on to new goals.

programmes.mediaNote: Photos and stories from meetings are shared on our Instagram — see link below.

programmes.title: Women’s Peer Support Group
programmes.teaser: Mondays at the Recovery Hub — crafts, activities, and connection in a women-only space.
programmes.referralNote: Open to clients registered with Recovery Near You or SUIT. No referral needed.

body.text: Our Women’s Peer Support Group runs every Monday at the Recovery Hub on Connaught Road, 12:30pm–2:30pm.
Come along and meet like-minded people while enjoying crafts and activities in a relaxed, supportive space.
The group is for clients registered with Recovery Near You or SUIT. For more information about this or any of our other groups, call SUIT on 01902 328983.

programmes.mediaNote: Group highlights and event posts appear on Instagram.

programmes.title: Men’s Peer Support — WV Men Matter
programmes.teaser: Friday group at the Recovery Hub — lived-experience facilitators building trust and connection.
programmes.referralNote: Open to anyone registered with SUIT or Recovery Near You. No referral necessary.

body.text: Our men’s group ‘WV Men Matter’ runs from the Recovery Hub on Connaught Road and is open to anyone registered with SUIT or Recovery Near You (no referral necessary).
Group connection is central to SUIT’s work. Our lived-experience team focuses on strength, resilience, and trust.
Facilitators Warren, Matt, Mark, and Jas bring their own experience of addiction and recovery — turning those challenges into knowledge to help others.
Our SUIT men are role models who start honest conversations without judgment. For more information call 01902 328983.

gallery.alt: Men’s group at the Recovery Hub

programmes.mediaNote: More photos and reels from the men’s group are on our Instagram.

programmes.title: RISE Day Hab Programme
programmes.teaser: An 8-week day programme with Recovery Near You — resilience, inspiration, strength, and experience.
programmes.location: Recovery Hub / programme venues (see your keyworker)
programmes.referralNote: Speak to your Recovery Near You keyworker about a referral. Small groups (up to 15) over 8 weeks.

body.text: RISE is an 8-week community day hab programme for people ready to live free from opiates, alcohol, or stimulants.
RISE stands for Resilience · Inspiration · Strength · Experience. It offers themed weekly sessions in a safe space to reflect, rebuild, and take back control.
What you’ll work on

items: Let go of guilt and shame
Learn practical tools for daily life

body.text: Across 8 weeks

items: Learn how to manage cravings and setbacks
Begin to see life beyond addiction

body.text: You’ll develop a personalised aftercare plan. Each day includes a warm check-in, morning and afternoon sessions, evidence-based support, and shared breaks.

Activities include mindfulness, park walks, and art and creative workshops. RISE is delivered with SUIT as the LERO alongside Recovery Near You — SMART & Day Hab Lead Warren works with RNY practitioners to blend lived experience and psychosocial support.
Lived experience service-user involvement working alongside clinical treatment is undoubtedly the most important ingredient for the optimum outcome in the battle against addiction.
Your choice. Your time. Your chance to rise.

programmes.title: Weekly Art Group
programmes.teaser: Tuesday creative sessions at Connaught Road — portfolios, exhibitions, and RISE collaborations.
programmes.location: Recovery Near You — Connaught Road Hub, Wolverhampton
programmes.referralNote: No formal referral needed; spaces can be limited. Speak to your RNY keyworker or email Christiane.

body.text: SUIT’s art collective meets every Tuesday, 12:30pm–2:30pm at Recovery Near You’s Connaught Road Hub. It’s ideal for beginners, and each week we welcome the RISE Day Hab programme for a meaningful creative session.

Sessions include guided practical work, portfolio pieces, exhibition planning, creative writing, exploring mediums, art history, and how styles emerge socially. The group is a safe space for discussion, planning, and individual support.
You don’t need art experience — bring motivation in your recovery and curiosity. Contact christiane.jenkins@wvca.org.uk or speak to your keyworker if you’d like to attend.

Working with established creatives, galleries, and festivals helps keep SUIT’s vision alive: real opportunities in spaces that can feel out of reach when you’re facing addiction and multiple disadvantage. Gallery photos below by Graham Stubbs at Wolverhampton Art Gallery.

gallery.alt: Art group at Wolverhampton Art Gallery
